nodes.label: License Activation Flow
nodes.rationale: The activation flow collects NIT (company tax ID), company name, and license type (commercial or trial/30-day), then binds the license to the server MAC address. This server-binding ensures the license cannot be reused on other machines.

nodes.label: License Tamper and Clock-Skew Detection
nodes.rationale: Two invalidation reasons are explicitly modelled: 'tampered' (license file corrupted or modified) and 'clock' (system clock was rolled back to bypass expiry). Both trigger forced re-activation, preventing offline time-manipulation attacks.

nodes.label: License State Machine (active/expired/tampered/clock-skew)
nodes.rationale: The system explicitly models at least three terminal states needing re-activation: normal expiry, file tampering, and clock manipulation. All redirect to the activation form, centralising recovery in one flow.

hyperedges.label: License Activation UI Flow (activate → success / expired)
